name: Name
workers_pinned_project_hint: This site stays selected for each worker while you use this link. Open Workers from the menu to choose the project every time.
end_date_optional_hint: Optional. Leave blank if unknown — set status to Completed when the project ends.
finance_expense_material_only_hint: Labour cost comes from attendance. Use this list for cement, sand, and other material expenses only.
finance_expense_hint_updated: Material expenses (cement, sand, brick, etc.) and contract (theka) bills. Labour from attendance stays separate.
contract_trade_required: Choose a contract type for this contractor.
cost_contractor_hint: Daily rate optional for contractors (can be 0).
trade_name_min: Trade name must be at least 2 characters
confirm_delete_trade: Delete this trade? Only allowed if unused.
roles_theka_section_hint: Used for contractor workers and when recording Contract / Theka expenses under project finance — e.g. Color, Furniture, Marble, Plumbing.
no_trades_in_roles_yet: Add theka sub-types on the Roles page first (Theka work types section).
roles_theka_empty: No sub-types yet — tap a quick type above.
contractor_role_hint: Choose Contractor to pick a theka sub-type. If you do not see Contractor, tap “+ Contractor” to create that role.
contractor_role_create_failed: Could not add the Contractor role. Try again or add it on the Roles page.
trade_duplicate_name: This work type name already exists. Use a different name.
role_duplicate_name: This role name already exists. Use a different name.
contract_expense_modal_hint: Records project cost and adds amount owed to the contractor’s ledger.
contract_expense_select_work_type: Please select a work type (sub-category) first.
no_contractors_for_trade: No contractor has this work type. Add or edit a contractor worker and assign this sub-type.
no_contractors: No contractors yet. Add a worker and set type to Contractor.
trade_override_hint: Override only if this bill is for a different type of work.
project_attendance_subtitle: Search workers and mark attendance for this project only — no project picker.
project_attendance_site_locked: This site is fixed for this page. Use Workers to mark on another site.
attendance_page_subtitle: See who is present or absent for the date you choose. You cannot edit the list on this screen.
attendance_mark_helper: To mark or change attendance, use Mark attendance above (opens Workers), or open Workers from the menu, select a worker, then tap Attendance.
attendance_no_records_project: No attendance for this project on this date.
half_day_split_hint: Total day wage is split 50/50 between both projects. Payments are split the same way.
second_project_required: Please select a second project (must differ from the current site).
ledger_you_owe: This is what you still owe the worker (after payments).
ledger_advance_paid: You have paid more than owed (advance).
ledger_project_help: This payment is added to that project’s labour expense.
ledger_project_required: Choose a project so the payment appears in expenses.
ledger_payment_no_project_hint: This only records cash or transfer paid to the worker in their ledger. Project labour cost already comes from attendance — it is not added again here.
finance_delete_body: This removes the transaction from the project. You cannot undo this.
install_android_hint: If you do not see the option, update Chrome or try again after using the site for a short while.
install_desktop_hint: Use your browser’s menu to install or add this site as an app.
install_fallback_intro: No install popup appeared (browsers only allow that when they’re ready). Add the app from your browser menu:
guide_page_subtitle: Pictures are taken from the real app. Read the short story under each title, then follow the numbered steps. You do not need to be good at English — read slowly.
guide_tip_screenshots: These pictures are from the real app. Scroll through each section and follow the numbered steps on your phone.
guide_footer_note: If something fails (red message on screen), read it aloud to your supervisor or the person who installed the app.
guide_box_how_to_read_body: Each part has: a title, a simple explanation, one phone screenshot, then numbered steps. Stop after each step and check your own phone matches.
guide_s01_intro: This is the gate to your data. Only people who know the password can enter. New users must use “Register” first (next section).
guide_s01_steps:
Open the app link your office gave you.
Type the 10-digit mobile number you used when you signed up.
Type your password (the secret word, at least 6 letters).
Press the Login button.
If it says “invalid phone or password”, check the number and password and try again.
guide_s02_intro: Use this only once, when your phone number is not in the system yet. After success you land on the Dashboard automatically.
guide_s02_steps:
Tap “Register” on the login page.
Type your full name (as you want workers to see it).
Type your own 10-digit mobile number.
Choose a password and type it twice the same way.
Press Register and wait until the Dashboard opens.
Write your password in a safe place at home — the app cannot show it again.
guide_s03_intro: Think of this as the day-board: how much money moved, how many sites are active, and attendance at a glance.
guide_s03_steps:
After login you should already be here.
Read the big rupee (₹) numbers — usually green for income, red/orange for spending.
Check “projects on site” or similar lines to see running work.
Use this screen every morning for a quick status check.
guide_s04_intro: The three-line button ☰ opens a drawer. Everything important is listed there, so you never get lost.
guide_s04_steps:
Tap ☰ on the top-left.
Read the list: Dashboard, Attendance, Projects, Workers, Roles, Transactions, How to use, Profile.
Tap any row to jump — the drawer closes by itself when you pick a page.
Come back to “How to use” whenever you forget a step.
guide_s05_intro: Before workers, list your job words here (Labour, Contractor…) and package-work names (Plumbing, Color…). Workers and bills will reuse these exact words.
guide_s05_steps:
Open Roles from the menu.
Under “quick add”, tap Labour (and other roles you really use).
Scroll to the yellow Theka section and tap quick types like Color if you use contractors.
Do not worry about spelling — but do not repeat the same name twice.
Leave this screen once the words you need are visible in the list.
guide_s06_intro: Each row is one construction site or job. From here you edit basics or open money (finance) for that site.
guide_s06_steps:
Open Projects from the menu.
Use the pills (Running / Completed / Small / Medium / Big) to filter.
Use the search line if you have many names.
The plus (+) button adds a new project (we cover that next).
guide_s07_intro: Give the site a clear name your team will recognise. Type and start date are the minimum the app needs here.
guide_s07_steps:
From Projects, tap Add / plus.
Type a clear project name (for example “Sharma Ji GK-2”).
Pick the start date on the calendar.
Choose size type: Small, Medium, or Big.
Tap Save. If a red message appears, read it — usually a field is empty.
guide_s09_intro: This is the money diary for one project only: money received, material bought, and Theka (contract) bills.
guide_s09_steps:
Tap Finance on a project card.
Use the three tabs: Summary, Income, Expense.
In Expense you can add Material (cement, sand…) or Contract/Theka (contractor work).
Income: fill amount, date, cash/online.
Always tap Save at the bottom of each form — if you do not save, money is not stored.
guide_s10_intro: Workers are the people you pay. Each card can mark attendance, open ledger (his account), or edit details.
guide_s10_steps:
Open Workers from the menu.
Use search if the list is long.
Green “Active” means you still employ that person.
To add someone new, tap the Add worker button (covered next).
guide_s11_intro: Save name, phone, daily wage, and job role. Contractors also need a Theka sub-type (Plumbing, etc.).
guide_s11_steps:
Tap photo area only if you have a clear face photo (optional).
Type worker name and 10-digit phone (different from yours).
Tap the correct role button (Labour, Karigar, Contractor…).
If Contractor: open the Theka drop-down and pick (or add) the work type.
Type cost per day in ₹ and tap Save.
guide_s14_intro: From the Workers list you can mark one person’s attendance. Attendance report in the menu is view-only; marking is done here on each worker’s card.
guide_s14_steps:
Open Workers from the menu or bottom bar.
Find the worker and tap Attendance (green calendar) on their card.
Choose the project and date.
Tap Present or Absent.
Choose Full day, Half day, or Other.
Check Day salary — it fills from their cost per day (you can adjust if needed).
Turn on Pay money? only if you are recording a payment with this attendance.
Tap Save attendance.
guide_s15_title: 12. Worker ledger — all transactions for one person
guide_s15_intro: Here you see every salary line (what is owed) and every payment for that worker only. Current balance is what you still owe after payments. Use this before handing over cash.
guide_s15_steps:
On Workers, open someone’s row and tap Ledger (blue book icon).
Read Current balance at the top — what remains to pay this person.
Add earning — add or adjust money still owed (e.g. salary from attendance).
Record payment — when you pay cash or transfer; it reduces the balance.
Scroll the list: OWED / Salary lines (green, plus) increase what is due; PAID / Payment lines (red, minus) show money already given.
Dates and amounts should match your site register.
You can still open Attendance report or Transactions for full-site views.
